items.author: Jon Hamm
items.body_excerpt:
My friend @fredkatz.bsky.social has been writing a book about the Knicks for over a year. Please pre-order his book because I promise you it will be so good:

www.simonandschuster.com/books/Under-...

media.title: Under the Bright Lights
media.description: The inside story of how the New York Knicks got back to NBA glory after being stuck in the league’s cellar for nearly two decades.From 1988 to 2001...

items.title: ANNOUNCEMENT: Under The Bright Lights, my upcoming book about how the Knicks built a champion, is now AVAILABLE FOR PREORDER!
items.author: Fred Katz
items.body_excerpt:
ANNOUNCEMENT: Under The Bright Lights, my upcoming book about how the Knicks built a champion, is now AVAILABLE FOR PREORDER!

I've spent years working on the book, which will chronicle never-before-told stories with a heavy focus on the title run.

Link: www.simonandschuster.com/books/Under-...

items.title: The public is invited to a special event at Bravuro Cellars winery from 2-4 p.m. Saturday featuring Mike Parker, Mike Riley, Craig Hanneman & Kerry Eggers. Parker will serve as moderator, with Eggers' new book "Scrolls of a Sports Scribe" featured. Join for an afternoon of wine &
items.author: Kerry Eggers
items.body_excerpt: The public is invited to a special event at Bravuro Cellars winery from 2-4 p.m. Saturday featuring Mike Parker, Mike Riley, Craig Hanneman & Kerry Eggers. Parker will serve as moderator, with Eggers' new book "Scrolls of a Sports Scribe" featured. Join for an afternoon of wine & sports & book talk.

items.title: Suns found an identity last season. This summer is about building around Devin Booker while sticking to that identity. No need to rush to an all-in move. Been there, done that. Didn't go great. Exercising some patience is the way forward this time around.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Suns found an identity last season. This summer is about building around Devin Booker while sticking to that identity. No need to rush to an all-in move. Been there, done that. Didn't go great. Exercising some patience is the way forward this time around.

More thoughts at the link ⬇️

items.title: Sad Bonus Hollander POTD Carey Scurry. Brooklyn hooper went to LIU & was drafted 37th by UTA in 85. Hustler & good leaper lasted 3 yrs as a bench guy (5 ppg 3 rpg). After 88, played abroad thru 97. Had struggles after retiring but got it together and wrote a book about it. RIP.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Sad Bonus Hollander POTD Carey Scurry. Brooklyn hooper went to LIU & was drafted 37th by UTA in 85. Hustler & good leaper lasted 3 yrs as a bench guy (5 ppg 3 rpg). After 88, played abroad thru 97. Had struggles after retiring but got it together and wrote a book about it. RIP.

items.title: Apparently Mitch Johnson may have been in the wrong here. The rule book states you have to call a timeout and then give the challenge signal in the same interaction with the ref. One can’t just twirl a finger to initiate a challenge and Nate is saying in replays he never actually
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt: Apparently Mitch Johnson may have been in the wrong here. The rule book states you have to call a timeout and then give the challenge signal in the same interaction with the ref. One can’t just twirl a finger to initiate a challenge and Nate is saying in replays he never actually called timeout.
